verses.text: God forbids Jeremiah to marry or have children here, for such shall die grievous deaths in the coming ruin. His celibacy is a sign. The prophet's own life preached the desolation that was near.
God forbids Jeremiah to marry or have children here, for such shall die grievous deaths in the coming ruin. His celibacy is a sign. The prophet's own life preached the desolation that was near.

He must not enter the house of mourning, for God has 'taken away my peace from this people'; none shall lament the dead. Even grief will be too great for ritual. The coming sorrow will overwhelm all custom.

He must not enter the house of mourning, for God has 'taken away my peace from this people'; none shall lament the dead. Even grief will be too great for ritual. The coming sorrow will overwhelm all custom.

He must not enter the house of mourning, for God has 'taken away my peace from this people'; none shall lament the dead. Even grief will be too great for ritual. The coming sorrow will overwhelm all custom.

Nor may he join the house of feasting, for God will silence 'the voice of mirth, and the voice of gladness.' Joy is to cease. A people under judgment will lose both their mourning and their merrymaking.

Nor may he join the house of feasting, for God will silence 'the voice of mirth, and the voice of gladness.' Joy is to cease. A people under judgment will lose both their mourning and their merrymaking.

When they ask why, the answer is plain: their fathers forsook God, and they have done worse, so they shall be cast into a strange land. Their guilt is inherited and increased. Exile is the wage of generations of rebellion.

When they ask why, the answer is plain: their fathers forsook God, and they have done worse, so they shall be cast into a strange land. Their guilt is inherited and increased. Exile is the wage of generations of rebellion.
Yet days come when men will say not 'the LORD that brought Israel out of Egypt,' but out of the north country. A greater deliverance is promised. God's future mercy will outshine even the exodus.
Yet days come when men will say not 'the LORD that brought Israel out of Egypt,' but out of the north country. A greater deliverance is promised. God's future mercy will outshine even the exodus.
God will send fishers and hunters to find them out, and recompense their iniquity double. None shall escape. The God who sees every hidden sin will repay it fully.
God will send fishers and hunters to find them out, and recompense their iniquity double. None shall escape. The God who sees every hidden sin will repay it fully.
God will send fishers and hunters to find them out, and recompense their iniquity double. None shall escape. The God who sees every hidden sin will repay it fully.
Jeremiah foresees the Gentiles confessing, 'our fathers have inherited lies,' as God makes them know His name. The nations will turn. The gospel will reach those once lost in idolatry.
